releaseNotes:
_Date: 2026-04-17_

---

### ✨ What's New

#### 🖍 Syntax Highlighting & Enhanced Tool Display
Code blocks in the chat are now **syntax highlighted** using GitHub-style light/dark themes (powered by `rehype-highlight`), making it far easier to read code snippets, scripts, and structured output at a glance. A new reusable **CodeBlock component** adds a language label and a one-click **copy button** to every code block.

Tool call results have been significantly improved too — the ACP tool display now covers a much wider range of tool types including **git, filesystem, scheduling, plan, and process tools**. When available, the tool's own description is surfaced for write/edit/delete operations so you always know exactly what an agent is doing. The scheduler's **OutputViewer** header has also been redesigned with a repositioned copy button and richer metadata display.

#### 🗒 Obsidian MCP Integration
A new **Obsidian server** entry is available in the MCP gallery (with official logo), letting you connect your Obsidian vault directly as a knowledge source. MCP server definitions now support an optional **info URL** field — when present, an info icon appears next to the tool name so you can jump straight to its documentation or project page.

#### ⚙️ Dual-Scope MCP Config Management
MCP server configuration now supports **two independent scopes — global (`~/.compass/.mcp.json`) and project-level** — giving you granular control over which tools are active per workspace versus across the entire app. Key features include:

- **Scope selector** in the MCP Config Dialog to choose where a server gets installed (global or project)
- **Scope badges and conflict warnings** on tool cards in the gallery
- **Scope filter tabs** (All / Global / Project) in the gallery header for quick filtering
- **Conflict detection** — project-level entries override global ones on name conflict (shallow merge), with visual warnings surfaced in the UI
- A dedicated **Zustand store** (`mcp-config.store`) handles all scope state and merging logic

#### 💤 Idle Mode & Hardware Acceleration Toggle
Building on the Performance Settings introduced in v1.0.2, two new power controls are now available:

- **Idle Mode** — a new `IdleManager` service automatically suspends non-critical services (WebSocket, Scheduler, WhatsApp) after **30 seconds of window inactivity**. The tray icon shows an `(Idle)` suffix and a "Resume from Idle" menu item so you always know the app's state.
- **Hardware Acceleration Toggle** — enable or disable GPU hardware acceleration from Settings. Changes take effect on next launch, with a **restart prompt** shown immediately when you flip the toggle. The setting is persisted to `performance.json` and read before `app.whenReady()` so it applies at the earliest possible startup point.

---

### 🛠 Improvements

- **Shared markdown rendering** — a new `markdownComponents` module is now used consistently across `ChatMessage`, `AssistantResponseCard`, and `CommandOutput`, eliminating duplicated rendering logic and ensuring a uniform look everywhere.
- **Multi-file upload in Knowledge** — `KnowledgeList` now supports selecting and uploading multiple files in one go.
- **MCP gallery discoverability** — server entries surface documentation links (info URL) inline, right next to the tool name.
- **MCP scope filter** — the gallery's scope filter correctly excludes tools not installed in the selected scope, so the view always reflects reality.
- **MCP auth status detection** — the auth status regex now correctly recognises the `✓` symbol and `no auth required` states.
- **Windows MCP shim fix** — `.cmd` shims are now resolved to their underlying executables before spawning, avoiding `cmd.exe` argument parsing issues on Windows.
- **FloatingChatMenu reordered** — Marketplace is now listed first, with utility actions grouped below a separator for a cleaner hierarchy.

---

_Co-authored by [Nova](https://www.compassap.ai/portfolio/nova.html)_
